name: worktree-guard
description:
PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for `git worktree add`. When a Bash command creates a git worktree, emits non-blocking additionalContext with the durable-worktree drill: in a heavily-concurrent or synced repo an isolated worktree dir can be removed out from under you (a sibling agent's `git worktree prune`, a workspace sync, or the macOS /tmp reaper), and any UNCOMMITTED edits vanish with it — so commit + push promptly after editing, don't hold uncommitted work across turns, and land via PR then `git worktree remove` (verification builds AFTER the commit, so a failed build never costs the work). Adds a stronger note when the target path sits under a volatile location (/tmp · /private/tmp · /var/folders) that is both tmp-reaped and outside the synced workspace. BRANCH-REUSE advisory (0.2.0, anima #1105): when the command is `git worktree add -b
` and `
` already exists — especially a LOCAL-only stale branch absent from origin (a rejected/unpushed prior turn's leftover) — warns that reusing it carries the stale-base risk that revert-deleted 35190 files in anima #1105, and suggests a fresh per-PR branch (`
-$(date +%s)` cut from origin/main). Implemented in hexa-lang (`_worktree_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Advisory-only by design (per the guards-narrow-scope policy: non-blocking additionalContext beats a deny for hygiene). Sister of workdir-guard (shared-tree SessionStart advisory) and worktree-gc (merged-worktree prune). NO opt-out by design.
